questions.question: The enzyme Amylase is present in:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Saliva contains enzyme Amylase which converts starch into maltose.

questions.question: Saliva converts starch into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Saliva (Amylase) converts Starch into Maltose.

questions.question: Gastric juice contains which enzymes?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Gastric juice contains Pepsin and Rennin.

questions.question: Pepsin in gastric juice converts:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Pepsin converts Protein into Polypeptides.

questions.question: Rennin in gastric juice converts:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Rennin converts Casein (milk protein) into Calcium paracasein.

questions.question: Pancreatic juice contains:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Pancreatic juice contains Trypsin (Protein→Sugar), Amylase (Starch→Amino acid and glycerol), and Lipase (Fat→Fatty acid and glycerol).

questions.question: Intestinal juice contains:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Intestinal juice contains Erepsin, Maltase, Lactase, Sucrase, Lipase etc.

questions.question: Maltase converts Maltose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Maltase converts Maltose into Glucose.

questions.question: Sucrase converts Sucrose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Sucrase converts Sucrose into Glucose and Fructose.

questions.question: Lactase converts Lactose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Lactase converts Lactose into Glucose and Galactose.

questions.question: Lipase converts fat into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Lipase converts Fat into Fatty acid and Glycerol.

questions.question: Erepsin converts:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Erepsin in intestinal juice converts remaining protein (peptones) into amino acids.

questions.question: Enzymes are:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Enzymes are biocatalysts.

questions.question: The root of tooth is covered by:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Root of tooth is covered by cementum.

questions.question: The pouch of caecum is connected to:

options.B: Junction of small and large intestine

questions.explanation: Page 414: The pouch (caecum) is connected to the junction of small and large intestine.

questions.question: Complete digestion of food takes place in:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Complete digestion of food takes place in small intestine.

questions.question: Digestion of fat takes place in:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Digestion of fat takes place in small and large intestine.

questions.question: Small intestine is longer than:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Small intestine is longer than large intestine.

questions.question: Undigested food reaches into large intestine where bacteria turns it into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Undigested food reaches large intestine where bacteria turns it into faeces, which is excreted.

questions.question: Absorption of digested food takes place through:

options.B: Wall of small intestine into blood

questions.explanation: Page 414: Absorption of digested food gets mixed into blood through the wall of small intestine.

questions.question: Intestinal juice is alkaline in nature. In healthy people approximately how many litres are secreted daily?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Intestinal juice is alkaline. In healthy people approximately 2 litres of intestinal juice is secreted everyday.

questions.question: The liver converts the emulsified fats into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver converts the emulsified fats into glycerol and fatty acid.

questions.question: Liver converts the maltose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver converts maltose into glucose.

questions.question: Liver converts the lactose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver converts lactose into glucose and galactose.

questions.question: Liver converts the sucrose into:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver converts sucrose into glucose and fructose.

questions.question: The liver is the largest gland in the human body. Its weight is approximately:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver is the largest gland of the human body. Weight is approximately 1500 grams.

questions.question: Bile is secreted from:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile is secreted from liver.

questions.question: Liver converts excess of amino acid into ammonia by:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver converts excess of amino acid into ammonia by deamination.

questions.question: Ammonia is further converted into urea by:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Ammonia is further converted into urea by ornithine cycle. Urea comes out of body through kidney.

questions.question: Bile of liver accelerates the action of:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile accelerates the action of enzymes present in intestine.

questions.question: The main functions of bile include making the pH of food as:

options.B: Alkaline and facilitates action of bile

questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile makes pH of food alkaline and facilitates action of enzymes.

questions.question: Bile neutralizes the acidic pH of food coming from:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile neutralizes the acidic pH of food coming from stomach.

questions.question: The pH value of bile is:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile pH value is 7.7.

questions.question: Bile contains approximately what percentage of water?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile contains 85% water and pigments 12%.

questions.question: Bile is a yellowish-green coloured and:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile is a yellowish-green coloured and alkaline liquid. Its pH is 7.7.

questions.question: Bile comes from the bile duct into the:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Bile comes from the bile duct into the duodenum.

questions.question: Gall bladder of liver is a:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Gall bladder of liver is a pear shaped sac. Bile is stored here.

questions.question: Liver is an important organ in regulating body:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver is an important organ in regulating the body temperature.

questions.question: Liver helps in investigation of a person's death due to:

options.B: Poisoning (it has been done due to investigating a person's death)

questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver helps in investigating a person's death (especially in poisoning cases).

questions.question: Dead RBCs are destroyed by:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Dead RBCs are destroyed by the liver.

questions.question: Liver reserves some quantity of:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver reserves some quantity of iron, copper and vitamins.

questions.question: Liver produces which protein that helps in clotting of blood?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver produces Fibrinogen protein (clotting) and Heparin (anti-clotting).

questions.question: In case of obstruction in bile duct, liver cells stop working. Bilirubin from blood spreads throughout the body causing:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Obstruction in bile duct → bilirubin spreads → Jaundice.

questions.question: Pancreatic juice secretes out of pancreas. The liquid's pH value is:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Pancreatic juice pH is 7.5-8.3. It contains 98% water and enzymes which can digest all three types of food.

questions.question: Pancreas is the second largest gland of the human body. It functions as both:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Pancreas is the second largest gland, functions as both exocrine (digestive juice) and endocrine (hormones).

questions.question: Islets of Langerhans are present in:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Islets of Langerhans are part of the Pancreas.

questions.question: Insulin is secreted by which cells of Islets of Langerhans?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Insulin is secreted by β-cells of Islets of Langerhans.

questions.question: Glucagon is secreted by which cells of Islets of Langerhans?
questions.explanation: Page 414: Glucagon is secreted by α-cells. Somatostatin hormones are secreted from δ-cells.

questions.question: Insulin was discovered by:
questions.explanation: Page 414: It was discovered by the medical scientist Langerhans.

questions.question: In case of excess glucose in blood, liver converts glucose into glycogen. If not, it converts to:
questions.explanation: Page 414: Liver regulates blood glucose: excess → glycogen → if more excess → fat.
